que: What is a companion object in Kotlin commonly used for?
How can companion object members usually be accessed?
If a companion object has no explicit name, what default name does Kotlin use?
Which is a common good use case for a companion object?
options: Managing every global concern in the app

que: Which statement about companion objects is correct?
options: They are singleton objects associated with the class
They are plain Java static blocks

que: Can a companion object implement an interface?
What is a common mistake with companion objects?
options: Treating them as dumping grounds for unrelated global behavior

que: When might a top-level function be better than a companion object member?
options: When the behavior is not meaningfully tied to a specific class
class User { companion object { fun create() = User() } }
class User { static fun create() = User() }
class User { object companion fun create() = User() }
